_source: Constructed from the OpenAI Agents SDK Runner.run() output (RunResult) and the Responses API state model. Trace structure mirrors what the SDK exposes via result.raw_responses + result.new_items + result.input_guardrail_results + result.output_guardrail_results. The SDK supports first-class tracing visualized in the OpenAI dashboard.

turns.content: I'll look up the order and customer details, then process the refund.

turns._note: Sample guardrail enforces that refunds > $200 require human approval. Tripwire halts execution; SDK returns the partial RunResult with the planned action visible for human review.

raw_responses: [Reference to underlying OpenAI Responses API response objects, accessible via result.raw_responses]
_note_on_final_output: Because a guardrail tripwire fired, the run halted before completion. The SDK's RunResult.interruptions field exposes the approvals_from_state — what the agent was about to do, so a human reviewer can approve, modify, or deny.

tracing.spans_exported_to: OpenAI dashboard tracing UI + optional custom exporters
tracing.span_data: Every model call, tool call, guardrail check, and handoff produces a span with timing, inputs, outputs

session_persistence.mechanism: Responses API state — pass previous_response_id to continue a conversation; the server reconstructs context. Alternatively, store messages locally and pass them back.

_notes_on_authenticity: Trace SHAPE is from the Agents SDK source (src/agents/run.py and src/agents/result.py).
Specific tool names and arguments are illustrative — OpenAI does not publish a per-customer sample trace publicly.
Per-turn token usage is from the Responses API documented response format.
Guardrail tripwire mechanism is verbatim from src/agents/guardrail.py — tripwire_triggered: bool field that halts execution.
